description: Labeled chunk examples for classifier regression eval (pnpm classify:eval). Add a new entry whenever a real sandbox run surfaces a misclassification — see DEBUG-LOG.md for the false-positive incident these first entries came from.

examples.text: Elm Street Drainage Project CDBG - Disaster Recovery Project Ordinance Nikki Stanton, Finance Officer
examples.note: Real candidate — named drainage capital project with a budget officer attached.

examples.text: Southern Nash Middle School Capital Project Ordinance Amendment Nikki Stanton, Finance Officer
examples.note: Real candidate — named capital project ordinance amendment.

examples.text: Juneteenth, a combination of the words "June" and "nineteenth," commemorates June 19, 1865, when Union Major General Gordon Granger announced General Order No. 3 in Galveston, Texas.
examples.note: Real prose content but not infrastructure/capital work — correctly skipped in the original run, kept as a true-negative regression check.

examples.text:
## Commissioner's Agenda Information Sheet

Elm Street Drainage Project CDBG - Disaster Recovery Project Ordinance Nikki Stanton, Finance Officer
examples.note: What the FIXED chunking pipeline actually produces — boilerplate header merged forward with its real body content. Confirms the merge-forward fix doesn't dilute/suppress a true positive.

examples.text: ## June 15, 2026 Second Regular Monthly Board Meeting
examples.note: Bare meeting title with zero body — false positive in the original incident. The chunking fix (functions/analyzer/src/chunking.py) now merges this forward in the real pipeline so it should never reach the classifier alone. This entry tests the classifier prompt as a defense-in-depth backstop, not the chunking fix; currently still fails (the prompt has no rule for 'insufficient context, default to false'). Tracked but not yet fixed — see DEBUG-LOG.md.
Same as fragment-only-meeting-title-boilerplate — isolated template header, now unreachable via the fixed chunking path, kept as a classifier-prompt backstop check.

examples.text: ## Nash County Commissioner's Agenda Information Sheet
examples.note: Same template header, slightly different wording — isolated-fragment backstop check, not the chunking-level fix.

Pure punctuation signature-line divider. The chunking fix's noise filter (_is_noise_paragraph) now drops this before it ever reaches the classifier in the real pipeline. This entry tests the classifier prompt alone as a backstop; currently still fails.
